__doc__: ANNOTATED EXAMPLE content JSON for build_report.py. Copy this, rename to content.cn.json / content.en.json, edit the values, then: python3 scripts/build_report.py content.cn.json. Keys prefixed __ are comments; the renderer reads only known keys and ignores the rest, so __* keys are harmless at build time — still DELETE them in your real file so future key renames can't silently collide and the file stays readable. (Real working copies live next to the Cardiff report: cardiff-2026/content.cn.json / content.en.json.)
__lang: 'cn' or 'en'. Picks the built-in typography preset (fonts, sizes, quote glyph). CN uses Alibaba PuHuiTi; EN uses Montserrat. This is the ONLY thing that switches the whole look — you never touch CSS.
__output: output HTML filename, relative to this JSON's folder. Override with -o on the CLI.
__style: OPTIONAL. A dict of CSS-value overrides on top of the preset, e.g. {"hero_h1_size":"clamp(40px,7vw,80px)"}. Omit entirely unless you must deviate. See PRESET_CN/PRESET_EN keys in build_report.py for the full list.

head.__lang_attr: OPTIONAL <html lang=...> override. LEAVE IT OUT — it defaults zh-CN for cn / en for en, and build_pdf/build_longimage read <html lang> to pick the watermark language. A stale hardcoded value (e.g. zh-CN left in an EN copy) silently gives the EN report a CN text-only watermark.
head.keywords: ...OPTIONAL comma-separated SEO/social tags, e.g. 'QS 2027, World University Rankings, Study-Abroad'. Omit to leave the keywords meta empty...

topbar.__logo: Logo is AUTO by lang — leave it out. EN→uhomes.com only (uhomes-logo-red.svg); CN→3-brand combined 异乡好居｜异乡缴费｜异乡人才 (uhomes-cn-combined-logo.svg). The logo is EMBEDDED into the HTML as a data: URI, so the generated .html is self-contained — no svg needs to be shipped alongside. Override only for university/apartment reports via logo_src (single img, path relative to this JSON; embedded too; add logo_alt for its alt text) or logo_html (full custom markup).
topbar.issue:
卡迪夫市场观察
2026 年 6 月

hero.kicker: 留学租房数据洞察 · 内部决策版
hero.h1:
上涨的英国市场里，
卡迪夫，掉队的城
hero.__h1:
headline.
for line breaks, ... for the coral-red emphasis word (inv 8).
hero.__meta: exactly 4 hero meta cells: b = big neutral-ink number, span = small caption (these are plain facts, not up/down deltas — kept neutral).

__stats: the headline stat CARDS (brand review 2026-06-18, designer mockup): each card = a coloured circular ICON BADGE + big semantic number + bold title (span) + muted subtitle (sub). Exactly 4 (the layout is a 4-column strip; other counts render but break the grid rhythm — the builder warns). Colour is SEMANTIC by sign: leading + → growth green (↑ badge), leading − → decline red (↓ badge), UNSIGNED → neutral ink (bars badge). Override colour with "tone":"pos"|"neg"|"neutral" (e.g. a bad rank like #17 → "neg" to make it red). Override the glyph with "icon":"up"|"down"|"bars"|"globe"|"flat". "sub" is the optional second caption line. A trailing % in b is auto-shrunk. Don't mix a signed delta with a bare ratio ("7 / 8") in the strip — rewrite as a big count.

chapters.h2: 章节标题（column 时间对比示例）

blocks.__variant: CHART TYPE (default 'bar'). Match the form to the question: 'line'=trend over time; 'donut'=share/proportion ('pie' is an alias); 'column'=vertical bars for a time comparison; 'bar'=ranking (horizontal). Add "diverging":true to a bar chart so NEGATIVE values show (extend left in decline-red). Chapters 01–05 below demonstrate one variant each. See REFERENCE §Chart variants.
blocks.__mode: BAR HELPER MODE (variant 'bar'/'column' only). 'scale' (default): data-w=value/max*scale. 'percent': data-w=value, printed N%. 'raw_w': you give w+value_text, no math, for hand-tuned YoY-% bars.
blocks.__scale: only used by mode 'scale'; biggest bar maps to this width (~95). Default 95.
blocks.__bars: Each row: label + value. COLOUR IS SEMANTIC (4-colour system, brand review 2026-06-18): default = neutral grey; hl:true → THE highlighted/subject bar (brand #FF5A5F, bold label); up:true → growth green; neg:true OR value<0 → decline red (in a normal bar the width zeroes; in a 'diverging' bar it extends LEFT). value_text overrides the printed cell ('+22%','2.0万'). w = explicit data-w (ONLY for raw_w mode — in scale/percent it bypasses the math, the builder warns, and preflight FAILs on >100). DO NOT use the legacy style:'coral'/'sand'/'ink' classes — preflight WARNs. For 'line': rows are ordered points (hl=brand dot, down=red dot, up=green dot). For 'donut': rows are slices (hl = brand slice; up/down tint a slice green/red); add "center":{"value":"47%","label":"…"}.

blocks.__fig: OPTIONAL inline restrained number prepended to the FIRST paragraph (inv 5: this is the .fig style, NOT a giant .bignum billboard).
blocks.__style: OPTIONAL raw inline style on the .note div.
blocks.__ps: list of paragraph HTML strings. Inline <b> for emphasis.

chapters.__blocks: ordered list of blocks rendered inside the chapter. chart/note blocks auto-wrap in a single-column .cols container (inv 3: never left-right, chart on top / note below). Wrap a block in {"type":"group","blocks":[...]} to nest it in a bare <div> (matches the source's column wrappers; purely structural).
chapters.__pull_quote: OPTIONAL. Rendered after the chapter blocks (inv 9: coral quote mark + cite).

blocks.__note: line = ordered points; hl marks the subject dot (brand), down marks a decline dot (red), up marks a growth dot (green).

chapters.h2: 占比条 + 手调宽度示例（percent / raw_w）

blocks.ct: 转化率（percent 模式：data-w=value，打印 N%）
同比涨幅（raw_w 模式：宽度手调、文字另给）
各城市同比（负值向左伸出，红色）
blocks.__note: diverging requires real signed numeric value (left/right computed from max(|value|)); value_text is the printed label.

blocks.__items: the numbered recommendation list. h3 supports ... for coral emphasis. A chapter whose blocks are all 'acts' renders them at section level (no .cols wrapper).

footer.__brand: EN can use 'uhomes.com' to color '.com' coral. Injected as RAW HTML — brand names can be clickable links (live in HTML/PDF, not the long PNG).
footer.note: 数据来源... (可含 table_name) ...仅供内部决策参考。
footer.__note: footer fine print, rendered at 10px (inv 4). Inline <code> allowed.
